climate.rainfallSeasonality: Strongly seasonal — 77.7% of rainfall falls May to September

primary.description: Perth's eastern Hills suburbs (Mundaring, Kalamunda, Roleystone) sit within dense jarrah-marri forest, creating extreme bushfire risk with BAL-29 to BAL-FZ ratings. The 2016 Perth Hills fire destroyed 57 homes.

Winter cold fronts and summer thunderstorm systems produce damaging winds exceeding 100 km/h, causing widespread roof damage and tree falls. Perth receives an average of 15 severe thunderstorm days per year.
Intense winter rainfall events overwhelm Perth's stormwater systems, causing localised flooding along the Swan River and Canning River corridors, particularly in low-lying suburbs.

secondary.description: While rare, tropical cyclones occasionally track south to affect the Perth metropolitan area. Cyclone Alby in 1978 caused significant wind damage across the city.
Perth regularly experiences summer heatwaves exceeding 40°C, causing thermal expansion in roofing materials, air conditioning failures, and increased pipe stress.

risks.bushfireAttackLevel: BAL-29 for Hills suburbs (Mundaring, Kalamunda, Roleystone); BAL-LOW for metro
risks.floodZone: Low-moderate — Swan River corridor, Canning River catchment
risks.cycloneCategory: Region C (Perth metro — rare direct impact)

historicalEvents.description: Cyclone Alby tracked unusually far south, bringing destructive winds and heavy rain to the Perth metropolitan area. The storm caused widespread property damage, multiple fatalities, and triggered bushfires across the southwest.

A devastating bushfire destroyed 57 homes in the Perth Hills and Waroona district. Ember attack extended into suburban Mundaring and Kalamunda, requiring emergency make-safe and smoke damage restoration across hundreds of properties.

A fast-moving grassfire destroyed 37 homes in Toodyay, northeast of Perth. The fire highlighted the bushfire vulnerability of Perth's peri-urban fringe and the need for rapid restoration response in fire-affected communities.

Cyclone Seroja made landfall as a Category 3 system near Kalbarri, causing catastrophic damage in the Mid West region. While Perth metro avoided direct impact, associated storm activity caused property damage across northern suburbs.

suburbs.name: Cottesloe
suburbs.riskNote: Coastal erosion and storm surge risk

suburbs.name: Scarborough
suburbs.riskNote: Coastal wind and storm damage exposure

suburbs.name: Armadale
suburbs.riskNote: Bushfire risk from adjacent jarrah forest

serviceNotes.waterDamage: Perth's strongly seasonal rainfall means water damage claims peak dramatically during the May-September wet season. Older properties in the western suburbs with limestone foundations are particularly vulnerable to rising damp and water ingress during prolonged rain events.
serviceNotes.fireDamage: Perth's Hills suburbs face Australia's highest urban bushfire risk. Smoke damage from bushfires can travel 50+ kilometres, affecting properties well beyond the fire front. Specialist soot and smoke remediation is frequently required across the entire eastern metropolitan corridor.
serviceNotes.mouldRemediation: Despite Perth's dry summers, air conditioning condensation and poor ventilation in modern sealed buildings create year-round mould risk. Post-flood mould colonisation occurs rapidly in Perth's warm climate, requiring intervention within 24-48 hours.
serviceNotes.stormDamage: Winter cold fronts regularly produce destructive wind gusts across Perth, with coastal suburbs bearing the brunt. Emergency tarping and make-safe services are critical during the wet season to prevent cascading water damage from compromised roofing.
